en: For everything there is a season, and a time for every matter under heaven:
zh: 萬事都有定期，天下萬務都有定時：

en: a time to be born, and a time to die; a time to plant, and a time to pluck up what is planted;
zh: 生有時，死有時； 栽種有時，拔出所種的也有時；

en: a time to kill, and a time to heal; a time to break down, and a time to build up;
zh: 殺戮有時，醫治有時； 拆毀有時，建造有時；

en: a time to weep, and a time to laugh; a time to mourn, and a time to dance;
zh: 哭有時，笑有時； 哀慟有時，踴躍有時；

en: a time to cast away stones, and a time to gather stones together; a time to embrace, and a time to refrain from embracing;
zh: 拋擲石頭有時，堆聚石頭有時； 擁抱有時，避免擁抱有時；

en: a time to seek, and a time to lose; a time to keep, and a time to cast away;
zh: 尋找有時，捨棄有時； 保存有時，拋棄有時；

en: a time to tear, and a time to sew; a time to keep silence, and a time to speak;
zh: 撕裂有時，縫補有時； 靜默有時，講話有時；

en: a time to love, and a time to hate; a time for war, and a time for peace.
zh: 愛有時，恨有時； 戰爭有時，和平有時。

en: What gain has the worker from his toil?
zh: 作工的人在自己的勞碌上得到甚麼益處呢？

en: I have seen the business that God has given to the children of man to be busy with.
zh: 我看 神給予世人的擔子，是要他們為此煩惱。

en: He has made everything beautiful in its time. Also, he has put eternity into man's heart, yet so that he cannot find out what God has done from the beginning to the end.
zh: 他使萬事各按其時，成為美好；他又把永恆的意識放在人的心裡；雖然這樣，人還是不能察覺 神自始至終的作為。

en: I perceived that there is nothing better for them than to be joyful and to do good as long as they live;
zh: 我曉得人生最好是尋樂享福，

en: also that everyone should eat and drink and take pleasure in all his toil- this is God's gift to man.
zh: 人人有吃有喝，在自己的一切勞碌中自得其樂；這就是 神的恩賜。

en: I perceived that whatever God does endures forever; nothing can be added to it, nor anything taken from it. God has done it, so that people fear before him.
zh: 我知道 神所作的一切，都必永存，無可增添，無可減少； 神這樣作，為要使人在他面前存敬畏的心。

en: That which is, already has been; that which is to be, already has been; and God seeks what has been driven away.
zh: 現在有的，先前就有；將來有的，早已有了；因為 神使已過的事重新出現 。

en: Moreover, I saw under the sun that in the place of justice, even there was wickedness, and in the place of righteousness, even there was wickedness.
zh: 我在日光之下又看見：審判的地方有奸惡，維護公義的地方也有奸惡。

en: I said in my heart, God will judge the righteous and the wicked, for there is a time for every matter and for every work.
zh: 我自己心裡說：“ 神必審判義人和惡人，因為各樣事務、各樣工作都有定時。”

en: I said in my heart with regard to the children of man that God is testing them that they may see that they themselves are but beasts.
zh: 我自己心裡說：“至於世人， 神要試驗他們，使他們看見自己與牲畜無異。

en: For what happens to the children of man and what happens to the beasts is the same; as one dies, so dies the other. They all have the same breath, and man has no advantage over the beasts, for all is vanity.
zh: 因為世人所遭遇的與牲畜所遭遇的，都是一樣：這個怎樣死，那個也怎樣死，兩者的氣息都是一樣，所以人並不勝於牲畜。一切都是虛空。

en: All go to one place. All are from the dust, and to dust all return.
zh: 大家都到一個地方去，都出於塵土，也都歸回塵土。

en: Who knows whether the spirit of man goes upward and the spirit of the beast goes down into the earth?
zh: 有誰知道人的靈是往上升，牲畜的魂 是下降而入地呢？”

en: So I saw that there is nothing better than that a man should rejoice in his work, for that is his lot. Who can bring him to see what will be after him?
zh: 因此我看人最好是在自己所作的事上自得其樂，因為這也是他的分；誰能使他看見他自己死後的事呢？
